_comment: Real-photo test corpus for FaceKeep (ROADMAP Phase 2). These images are NOT
committed to the repo — download.py fetches them into a local cache and the
corpus tests skip when the cache is absent (e.g. offline / CI). All entries
are license-clear (Public Domain or CC-BY/CC-BY-SA with attribution recorded
below); the URL is a Wikimedia Commons Special:FilePath redirect (?width=800
keeps each file small). 'sha256' is the digest of the exact bytes that URL
served at capture time — download.py verifies it, so a silently changed file
fails loudly instead of corrupting a test. 'faces' is the count Haar detected
on the loaded image at capture (used to choose detection assertions, not as a

images.title: File:Official portrait of Barack Obama.jpg
images.attribution: Pete Souza, The Obama-Biden Transition Project

images.title: File:Albert Einstein Head.jpg
images.note: Grayscale (mode L) source — exercises the load path on a single-channel original.

images.title: File:Beatles ad 1965 just the beatles crop.jpg
images.note: Multi-face group — drives the 'detect several faces' assertion.

images.title: File:Migrant Mother (LOC fsa.8b29516).jpg
images.attribution: Dorothea Lange, U.S. Library of Congress
images.note: Large frame (3840x4929) with faces a small fraction of the image, so aggressive mode's downsample genuinely saves bytes (ratio>1) — unlike the 800px renders where faces fill the frame. Drives the aggressive ratio/restore-LPIPS regression lock (test_corpus_aggressive_regression.py). One large + one small/distant face, so region-local conservatism fires.
